message:
perf(storage): skip DDL/migration when SQLite schema is already current

When opening an existing database whose schema is already at
CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION, the full apply_schema() call (DDL statements,
index creation, migration checks) is now skipped entirely. This
eliminates unnecessary write-lock acquisition on read-heavy open
paths and allows concurrent readers to open the database even when
another process holds a write lock.

Changes:
- schema.rs: Set PRAGMA user_version to CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION at the
  end of apply_schema() so future opens can detect the version
- sqlite.rs: Check PRAGMA user_version before calling apply_schema();
  skip if already at the current version. Import CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION.
- Added test: open_with_timeout_does_not_require_write_lock_when_schema_current
  — verifies that opening an existing DB succeeds even while another
  connection holds BEGIN IMMEDIATE (write lock), confirming the
  read-only open path works

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
